title: Learning to Bake Sourdough During a Quiet Weekend

all.en: After two stressful weeks at work, I decided to spend the entire weekend baking sourdough.
all.zh: 經過兩週繁重的工作後，我決定整個週末都用來烤酸種麵包。

all.en: I had bought a small jar of sourdough starter from a local bakery the previous Friday.
all.zh: 上週五我從一家在地烘焙店買了一小罐酸種酵母。

all.en: The owner had warned me that sourdough was less about speed and more about listening.
all.zh: 店主曾警告我，做酸種麵包比起速度，更重要的是用心傾聽。

all.en: On Saturday morning, I cleared my entire kitchen counter and turned off my phone.
all.zh: 週六早上，我把整個廚房檯面清理乾淨，並關掉了手機。

all.en: I wanted to focus completely without any distracting notifications buzzing in my pocket.
all.zh: 我想完全專注，不要任何擾人的通知在口袋裡嗡嗡作響。

all.en: I gently fed the starter with flour and water, then watched it slowly come back to life.
all.zh: 我輕輕地用麵粉和水餵養酵母，看著它慢慢甦醒。

all.en: Tiny bubbles formed within an hour, rising through the surface like quiet underwater whispers.
all.zh: 一個小時內就形成了小氣泡，像水下安靜的耳語般從表面升起。

all.en: I mixed bread flour, water, and a pinch of salt in a large ceramic bowl.
all.zh: 我在一個大陶瓷碗裡混合了麵包粉、水和一小撮鹽。

all.en: The dough felt sticky and stubborn at first, refusing to come together smoothly.
all.zh: 麵團起初感覺黏膩又難搞，怎麼也揉不成漂亮的形狀。

all.en: I followed online instructions to stretch and fold the dough every thirty minutes.
all.zh: 我按照網路教學每三十分鐘將麵團拉伸並折疊一次。

all.en: Between folds, I made myself a strong cup of coffee and read a quiet novel.
all.zh: 在折疊的空檔，我為自己泡了一杯濃咖啡，並讀了一本安靜的小說。

all.en: By the afternoon, the dough had transformed into a soft, elastic, almost living thing.
all.zh: 到了下午，麵團已經變成柔軟、有彈性、幾乎像是有生命的東西。

all.en: I shaped it into a tight ball, dusted it with rice flour, and placed it into a basket.
all.zh: 我把它揉成緊實的圓球，撒上米粉，放進發酵籃裡。

all.en: Then the basket went into the refrigerator for a long overnight cold fermentation.
all.zh: 接著發酵籃被放進冰箱，進行漫長的隔夜低溫發酵。

all.en: I went to bed early, oddly excited about what the next morning would reveal.
all.zh: 我早早上床睡覺，對於隔天早上會發生什麼事感到出奇地興奮。

all.en: Sunday began with bright sunlight slipping through my bedroom curtains around seven.
all.zh: 週日清晨七點左右，明亮的陽光從我臥室的窗簾縫隙中灑了進來。

all.en: I pulled the basket from the fridge and admired the dough's risen, dimpled surface.
all.zh: 我把發酵籃從冰箱拿出來，欣賞著麵團膨脹後、布滿小凹痕的表面。

all.en: I preheated my heavy iron pot inside the oven until it was almost too hot to approach.
all.zh: 我在烤箱中預熱厚重的鐵鍋，直到熱到幾乎無法靠近。

all.en: Carefully, I tipped the cold dough onto a square of parchment paper.
all.zh: 我小心翼翼地把冷麵團倒在一張方形烘焙紙上。

all.en: Using a small blade, I scored a clean cross across the top of the round loaf.
all.zh: 我用一把小刀片在圓麵團頂部劃出一個乾淨的十字。

all.en: The scoring would let the bread expand beautifully as it baked inside the pot.
all.zh: 這個劃線可以讓麵包在鍋中烘烤時漂亮地膨脹。

all.en: I lowered the dough into the screaming hot pot and quickly slammed the lid shut.
all.zh: 我把麵團放進滾燙的鍋子裡，並迅速把蓋子蓋上。

all.en: The whole house slowly filled with the warm, sweet, comforting smell of baking bread.
all.zh: 整間屋子慢慢瀰漫著溫暖、香甜、令人安心的烘焙麵包香氣。

all.en: After twenty minutes, I removed the lid to let the crust turn a deep golden brown.
all.zh: 二十分鐘後，我打開蓋子讓麵包外皮烤成深金黃色。

all.en: The bread had risen into a beautiful round shape, with the cross opening like a flower.
all.zh: 麵包膨脹成漂亮的圓形，劃出的十字像花朵般綻開。

all.en: When I finally pulled it from the oven, the crust crackled softly as it cooled.
all.zh: 當我終於將它從烤箱取出時，麵包外皮在冷卻時發出輕柔的劈啪聲。

all.en: I forced myself to wait an hour before slicing, even though the temptation was huge.
all.zh: 我強迫自己等了一個小時才切，儘管誘惑實在太大。

all.en: The first slice revealed an open, airy crumb with shiny holes throughout the inside.
all.zh: 切下的第一片露出鬆軟、通氣的麵包心，內部布滿亮亮的氣孔。

all.en: I spread a generous layer of butter onto a warm slice and took a careful first bite.
all.zh: 我在一片溫熱的麵包上塗了厚厚一層奶油，小心翼翼地咬下第一口。

all.en: The crust was crunchy, the inside soft and slightly tangy, exactly as I had hoped.
all.zh: 外皮酥脆，內部柔軟而帶點微酸，正如我所期望的那樣。

all.en: I sent a photo to my mother, who immediately replied asking when I would visit her.
all.zh: 我傳了一張照片給媽媽，她立刻回訊問我什麼時候要去看她。

all.en: I promised to bake another loaf and bring it to her house the following weekend.
all.zh: 我答應下週末會再烤一條，帶到她家去。

all.en: That afternoon, I shared slices with two neighbors who happened to pass my open door.
all.zh: 那天下午，我把麵包片分給兩位剛好經過我家敞開大門的鄰居。

all.en: Both of them asked for the recipe, but I told them sourdough was about patience first.
all.zh: 他們兩位都向我要食譜，但我告訴他們酸種麵包最重要的是耐心。

all.en: I went to bed that night feeling calmer, prouder, and quietly ready for another week.
all.zh: 那晚我帶著平靜、自豪而靜謐的心情入睡，為下一週做好了準備。

all.en: The following Saturday, I baked two loaves and wrapped one in a clean linen cloth.
all.zh: 接下來的週六，我烤了兩條麵包，並用乾淨的亞麻布把其中一條包好。

all.en: I drove to my mother's house carrying the still-warm bread on the passenger seat.
all.zh: 我開車去媽媽家，把仍溫熱的麵包放在副駕駛座上。

all.en: She greeted me at the door, her glasses fogged up from the simmering pot inside.
all.zh: 她在門口迎接我，眼鏡因屋內熬煮的鍋子而蒙上一層霧氣。

all.en: We sat at the old kitchen table and tore the bread by hand, like we did long ago.
all.zh: 我們坐在那張老舊的廚房桌旁，像以前一樣用手撕著麵包。

all.en: She dipped her piece into a small bowl of olive oil with cracked black pepper.
all.zh: 她把麵包沾進一小碗加了現磨黑胡椒的橄欖油裡。

all.en: She told me my late grandfather had also baked bread on quiet Sundays in his youth.
all.zh: 她告訴我，外公年輕時也曾在安靜的週日烤過麵包。

all.en: I had never heard that story before and asked her to share every small detail.
all.zh: 我從未聽過這個故事，便請她分享每一個小細節。

all.en: We spent the whole afternoon flipping through dusty albums filled with kitchen photographs.
all.zh: 我們整個下午都在翻閱布滿廚房照片、灰塵滿佈的舊相簿。

all.en: Before I left, she handed me his old wooden bread paddle, kept safely in a closet.
all.zh: 我離開前，她把外公那把一直收在櫥櫃裡的老木製麵包鏟交給我。

all.en: I drove home holding it carefully, feeling that my baking journey had only just begun.
all.zh: 我小心翼翼地捧著它開車回家，深感自己的烘焙之旅才剛剛開始。

all.en: I cleaned the old wooden paddle that night and oiled it carefully under the warm kitchen light.
all.zh: 那晚我清潔了那把老木鏟，並在溫暖的廚房燈光下仔細上油保養。

all.en: The next morning, I used the paddle to slide a fresh dough into my heated iron pot.
all.zh: 隔天早上，我用那把木鏟把新揉好的麵團滑進預熱好的鐵鍋裡。

all.en: The bread baked beautifully, and somehow it tasted deeper, perhaps colored by my grandfather's history.
all.zh: 麵包烤得很漂亮，不知為何嚐起來更有層次，也許是被外公的故事染上了味道。

all.en: I photographed the loaf beside the paddle and sent the picture to my mother before lunch.
all.zh: 我把麵包與木鏟並排拍下，並在午餐前把照片傳給媽媽。

all.en: She replied with a heart and a single short line about how proud her father would be.
all.zh: 她回了一個愛心和一句簡短的話，說她爸爸看到一定會很驕傲。

all.en: I started baking every Saturday morning, slowly trying rye, whole wheat, and a soft sesame variation.
all.zh: 我開始每個週六早晨都烤麵包，慢慢嘗試裸麥、全麥，以及柔軟的芝麻變化款。

all.en: Neighbors began leaving thank-you notes inside my mailbox after I shared loaves around the building.
all.zh: 在我與大樓鄰居分享麵包後，他們開始把感謝的字條塞進我的信箱裡。

all.en: One older woman across the hall asked if I might teach her grandson to bake one quiet Sunday.
all.zh: 住對門的一位長者問我，是否能在某個安靜的週日教她孫子學烤麵包。

all.en: I agreed, and the boy arrived with curious eyes and a small, neatly tied checkered apron.
all.zh: 我答應了，那個男孩帶著好奇的眼神和一條繫得整齊的小格紋圍裙來到我家。

all.en: We baked together until evening, and the simple paddle now belonged quietly to a brand new generation.
all.zh: 我們一起烤到傍晚，那把樸素的木鏟如今悄悄地傳承到了全新的一代。
